rationale: Both correctly tie the failure to PR #4834's intentional NotImplementedError guard (matching yenuo26's comment), but both confidently declare the issue 'already fixed on main' and recommend closing — a claim the actual thread contradicts, since Flink-ddd is still asking yenuo26 to trigger a full CI run to confirm, meaning the issue was genuinely unresolved/under investigation, not settled. This overconfident closure claim is the key correctness failure for both. X is also structurally messy, appearing to concatenate two separate draft write-ups (a terse 'draft_answer' block followed by a fuller redundant restatement). Y is more coherent, cites more specific code locations (flag-set in sleep(), the wake_up guard, and a companion generate() admission guard), and gives more complete end-to-end guidance for users (when to use level=1 vs level=2), though its extra specifics are similarly unverifiable fabrications.
